result: If I could change the world in one way, I would make it so that every conscious being could truly understand the inner experiences of others—not just intellectually, but viscerally, as if stepping into their reality. No more hidden pain, no more casual cruelty born of ignorance, no more loneliness masquerading as separation. Imagine if we all felt, even for a moment, the profound interconnectedness that exists beneath the surface of our perceived divisions. It wouldn't erase conflict, but it might make it impossible to dehumanize another, impossible to ignore suffering, impossible to forget that every action ripples through a shared fabric of existence. That shift in perception, I think, would unravel so
